data_source: Dongfang Electric 2024 annual report (published 2025-04-30), 2025 interim report (published 2025-08-29), and market valuation data available through 2026-03-12

g4_revenue_trend.description: Revenue grew from RMB 55.4bn in 2022 to RMB 60.7bn in 2023 and RMB 69.7bn in 2024; no structural decline signal.

g5_gross_margin_trend.description: Gross margin fell to about 15.5% in 2024 from about 18.8% in 2023, but the data used does not show a 2+ year consecutive decline.

g7_moat.description: Dongfang Electric is a major state-backed power equipment supplier with real engineering depth, but this is still a project-driven capital-goods business. I cannot describe a decade-long moat that prevents peers such as Harbin Electric, Shanghai Electric, or global turbine OEMs from replicating enough of the offering to compress returns.

control_groups.conclusion: PASS — 2024 ROE is still below 12%, and the 2025-10-31 market PE implies a sub-6% earnings yield.
PASS — even weaker ROE and no valuation edge, so the control group does not improve the case.

buffett_action: N/A — live analysis, not a Berkshire historical backtest case
key_insight: Policy tailwinds and order growth are real, but current returns on capital and earnings yield are too weak for a Buffett-style hold.
